title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her mother.
Madison runs on behalf of Massachusetts General Hospital, crediting their care for extending her mom's life.
She became a nurse specializing in oncology, inspired by her mother's battle with brain cancer.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly experiencing a mental health crisis after he attacked officers with a sword.
The incident occurred near Northeastern University, continuing a pattern of recent violence in the area.
Boston EMS confirmed multiple non-life-threatening injuries among police and emergency responders during the altercation.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother, Diana.
Dubois aims to support families by donating to the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital.
She qualified for the Boston Marathon in September 2025 and is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a Brockton house fire early Saturday morning at 80 Glendale Ave.
Two other residents were hospitalized following the fire, which occurred around 12:30 a.m.
Investigators believe the fire began in a second-floor bedroom and is not considered suspicious.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire State Police on Saturday night.
Masse allegedly fired a long gun at family members and wounded a Nottingham officer that afternoon.
State police engaged Masse in the woods; he was killed during an exchange of gunfire with officers.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, 47, from Canton, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving breast cancer.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, also a cancer survivor and widow to a sarcoma victim, will run alongside her.
Both women are participating in the marathon to support Mass General and celebrate life, survivorship, and friendship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' on April 4, marking his fifth appearance, joining the show's five-timers club.
Jack White accompanied Black as musical guest, performing for the fifth time, including past appearances with The White Stripes.
Black's episode featured a cameo-filled opening and a performance of 'Seven Nation Army' with White and dancers.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past struggles with his left hand, attributing them to a 2021 wrist injury.
Brown underwent major surgery on his left wrist in 2021, re-injuring it in 2023, affecting his performance.
Since recovery, Brown improved his left-hand skills, achieving career-highs with 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, 5.3 assists per game.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Fenway Park with the San Diego Padres, reuniting with former NESN colleagues.
Orsillo, Red Sox play-by-play voice from 2001-2015, reflects on his tenure regarded as his dream job.
Despite leaving for the Padres in 2016, Orsillo maintains a fond connection with Boston fans and colleagues.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora explains using Roman Anthony as DH due to defensive struggles early this season.
Roman Anthony's offensive performance includes a triple and base hit in recent game against the Padres.
Anthony's defensive issues continue, recording an error on a throw, but shows improvement as noted by Cora.
